description: Geology Station 10 near the ALSEP Site

tokens: And I'm starting on the double core, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) And, John, since we're running behind here, I wonder if you can operate the penetrometer.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Possibly while he assembles the double core) A piece of cake.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Why don't you just give us an extension?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, how about an extension, you guys?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh, we understand and we can understand why you wouldn't want to get back in, but we'd like you to get back in on time.
And you've got a lot of science (done) there, so don't worry about it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You said all we was gonna do tonight is sit around and talk!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, we like to hear you talk.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Responding to Tony) Yeah, especially on a hot mike, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That just makes it more interesting.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Ten minutes (extension) and we could get all this done, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That bag number 4, see where it is?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Came off, (incredulous) and it's hanging between the fender and the frame.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, can I have your hammer?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Getting the go-ahead from Flight) Okay, we'll go ahead and give you 10 minutes.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Just shows that we love you.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Let's hear it for old Flight.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Want me to help you with the penetrometer?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, I've got the double core right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'll still drop the trench...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; well, this would be an opportunity for me...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I bet you I don't get this (double core) in here, but I'll try it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me do that, and you do the penetrometer because I know how to do that one.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, see if I got the red dot on my camera.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, how about spinning it once for me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll get it, (meaning a) cross-Sun (of the core tube) as far you (meaning Charlie, himself) can push it in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You want to hammer on this?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; now the stuff to...(Brief Pause while Charlie searches for a word)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Finishing the thought) Take it apart with.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...take it apart with, it's back in the back (of the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They want you to take both of them (meaning the coupled core sections) off (the extension handle) together and then ram it home before you separate the two.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And the top of the bottom one is back here next to the LSM (Lunar Surface Magnetometer).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, which...You want me to start with the 0.5?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right, it will be the 0.5...Well, actually you have the 0.2 on there.
Why don't you do the string of 0.2 (as shown in
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well; no, I took the 0.2 off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I don't have anything on here now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It gets hard down there, doesn't it, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I don't think it's going to go.
(To Tony) , "How many hits you want me to give it, Houston, before I quit.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, it's not going down at all?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, you're getting it a little bit, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's going in, John; about a quarter inch a stroke.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: No, a little bit more than that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, it's too late to change your mind; you got to hammer that one; no trenches.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Laughs) Should have kept my mouth shut.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It may have a bad latch in it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Now I'm on Intermediate cooling, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, John, you've probably hammered on that long enough.
Why don't we just call it enough.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: How far do you want to drive it, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Drive it all the way in?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, they don't want any more than that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Gee, it came right back out, too.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, John, you want to turn that over?
That stuff may come out of that.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I finally got that five-tenths (tip) back on.
And I got number 6 (position on the recording drum), Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Where are the core caps at, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm going to bypass 9 and go to 10.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, it's full in the bottom of it, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And that one should be fairly near the double core, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The core tube caps go on and everything.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, that bottomed it out, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Why don't we go to a 0.2 (tip) and do it just in the same place.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I've got almost...(Stops to listen) Okay.
(Pulling the penetrometer out) And then work towards the...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Is this the last time we use this thing (meaning the 0.5 tip)?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We don't have it on...On EVA-3, we don't use it, huh?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, I'll put it back, anyway.
(Pause) John, could you move over a skosh, let me...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, that rammer-jammer only went in an inch!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's okay, as long as it (meaning the keeper) gets out of the cap.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Isn't that the way it's supposed to go in?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, you got it so full...(Stops to listen to Tony) You probably got it so full...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You're going to leave that cap (meaning the top end closure) on it, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Until it gets back to Houston.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, where's the top thing that goes on it, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The one for the bottom is right there by the LSM (means LPM - the Lunar Portable Magnetometer), on top of the (tool) pallet.
And a cap goes on the (bottom of the) top part (meaning the upper section).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right by the LSM, by the LPM, there's a screw on (top end closure that's) just like on the top part.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That screws back in the (top of the) bottom part.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now ram that one (meaning the lower section), and then put a cap on the other one (meaning the upper section) and give them the numbers.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) The numbers of these things...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, the two-tenths almost went in all the way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the upper one was 45; the bottom one was (0)4.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Be advised I'm sorry that I'm spiking this thing out occasionally (by using a number of discrete pushes), but this is the only way we can do it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I've got two more to do, cycling to (drum position) 12.
And this is about a fourth of the way (from the Rover to the deep core site).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, we should wait on your pan until Charlie's through there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's about the same distance, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Passing on a request from Lovell in the Backroom) Hey, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...there, you might look around and see if you see any of that vesicular basalt.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's what I'm a looking for.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Telling a small fib) I told them you were.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We see that one went all the way in.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: But there it is, all but about 5 inches.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, John, stand by for a feedwater tone.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And I'm right by the double core.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The ground's pretty beat up with...(Stops to listen) Okay, but it's pretty beat up with footprints.
Should I go to a pristine area or stay here?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Just move it over out of the footprints.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: About 2 meters toward the Central station.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That one's going all the way in, too.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If I didn't lose my balance.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause as he retrieves the penetrometer) Recovery.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: One flat plate (measurement) by the double core, and that'll about do her.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony, (with regard to) that (heat flow) connector, the printed circuit on the heat flow is still in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It looked like the silver part broke off right where it's mated into the printed circuit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I just don't see any vesicular basalt.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's sort of like 'they're always there when you need them'.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Probably talking to the penetrometer) Crummy thing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's the flat plate, Tony, and I'm...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...that penetration, we can go ahead and take your pans.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And it's somewhere between this mass of footprints.
(Pause) Okay, Tony, right beside the double core.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Grunting) Okay, that was a pretty good one.
I think it's going to turn out; and it went in about (pause) 6 centimeters.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me try one out here in a pristine area to see if that's...A couple of meters away, here.
I wonder whether all that foot walking over there (at the double core site) might have just fouled that up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That one went in about the same.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We had that one on TV.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Can you see if my (Hasselblad) lens is dirty?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I've got to look in the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Which way do you want me to turn?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Towards the...(Pause) Slight (amount of) dust but not much, it's okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I collected one sample, which was a sharp, angular (rock).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we're going to have to pack up and head home.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Can you put this back there, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There, I thought we had lost this rake here for a minute, but there it is.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And that sample is going in bag sample 381, and I'll shoot the pan here in a second.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And it was a black rock; but I don't think it was vesicular basalt.
I think it was a breccia.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: In a lot of ways, it's the same type as we've been sampling it.
Going into sample bag (means SCB-)4 on top of the sample (means Station) 9 samples.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm up to frame count 91 on magazine Delta.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, John, I'm going to take that SCB number 2, my camera, and I'm heading home.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; that pan completes me up to frame of 89, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, John, when you get inside (means "get seated"), we have a (switch/circuit breaker) configuration change in LRV.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Charlie's already back at the LM.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We saw that before you went off (the air, meaning before John switched off the TV.)
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: He walks a lot faster than I do.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Except for the PWM Select and the Drive Enables, we would like everything back to nominal.
Circuit breakers in; and Drive Power on Bus Delta.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Circuit breakers are in, Drive Power's on Delta, and Steering's on Delta.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) And we understand you reset (the nav) before you came.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, looking back up...(Stops to listen) Yeah, I did that.
transcript.speaker: Duke
